prompt: I have a pre-generated simulation bank of 20,000 parameter-data pairs saved as .npz files in a directory. Each file contains 'parameters' (a vector of 3 values) and 'observables' (50 i.i.d. measurements). The original simulator is proprietary and I can't run it anymore. Train a BayesFlow amortized estimator using offline training. I need to hold out some data for validation and diagnostics.
assertions: Uses workflow.fit_offline() or workflow.fit_disk() (not fit_online, since no simulator is available)
Splits the simulation bank into training and held-out validation/test sets
Does NOT attempt to create or call a simulator function (it's unavailable)
Uses the naming shorthand (inference_variables=, summary_variables=) since no adapter transforms are needed, OR uses an explicit adapter — either is acceptable
Routes observables through summary_variables with a summary network (not inference_conditions)
Uses a SetTransformer for the i.i.d. observations
Passes validation_data= to fit_offline or fit_disk
Uses the held-out test set for compute_default_diagnostics (not the training data)
Checks diagnostics against house thresholds before declaring success
Notes the limitation that posterior predictive checks require access to the simulator, which is unavailable
